data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Fiverr International beats Q1, FY25 revenue outlook
European markets mixed as traders assess earnings, potential tariffs
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.34%

UK's inflation rises to 3% in January

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.39%

France (CAC:IND) -0.14%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) was little changed on Wednesday as markets assessed U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff talks, a host of earnings releases, and a hotter-than-expected U.K. inflation print.

Sectors and bourses were trading in mixed territory, after the benchmark's record close in the previous session.

On the corporate front, BAE Systems' (OTCPK:BAESF) shares slipped despite the British defense giant reporting a record order book, while Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) was trading lower after posting a year-on-year decline in adjusted earnings in its preliminary annual results.

Europe’s largest lender HSBC (HSBC) meanwhile reported an annual pre-tax profit of $32.31 billion on Wednesday and initiated a share buy-back of up to $2 billion.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points 4.56%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points at 2.52%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points 4.60%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: BAE Systems Non-GAAP EPS of 68.50P, revenue of £28.34B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BAE Systems plc press release (OTCPK:BAESY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of 68.50P.
  • Revenue of £28.34B (+14.0% Y/Y).
  • Free cash flow was £2,505M, reflecting a high level of customer advances towards the end of the year and strong operational cash conversion.
  • Order backlog grew by 11% to a record £77.8B, which included order backlog of £3.0B related to SMS.

data.title: Glencore GAAP EPS of -$0.13, revenue of $230.94B misses by $550M
data.description:
• Glencore press release (OTCPK:GLCNF): FY GAAP EPS of -$0.13.
  • Revenue of $230.94B (+6.0% Y/Y) misses by $550M.
  • $14.4 billion Adjusted EBITDA, down 16%, primarily reflecting weaker energy coal prices.
  • Funds from operations of $10.5 billion, up 11%.
  • Net cash purchase and sale of PP&E: $6.7 billion compared to $5.6 billion in 2023; EVR accounts for majority of the increase.
  • Adjusted EBITDA mining margins were 28% in our metals operations, 45% in steelmaking coal and 32% in energy coal.

data.title: Nexans S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Nexans S.A. (OTCPK:NXPRF): FY Net income at €283 million, up +27% versus 2023
  • Revenue of €7.1B (+8.7% Y/Y)
  • Full-year 2025 guidance announced o Adjusted EBITDA of between €770 and 850 million
  • Free Cash Flow of between €225 and 325 million
  • Net debt at €681 million and 0.85x leverage ratio

data.title: Daily Journal reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Daily Journal press release (NASDAQ:DJCO): Q1 effective tax rate for the three months ended December 31, 2024 was 26.9%, after including the taxes on the unrealized gains on marketable securities.
  • Revenue of $17.7M (+10.7% Y/Y).

data.title: New Zealand gets another 50-bp rate cut, more easing expected this year
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand has cut its benchmark interest rate by 50 basis points to 3.75%, in an effort to boost economic activity while inflation pressures continue to ease. This is its third 50-bp cut, with rates being lowered 175 bps since August, when it started its easing cycle.

New Zealand's economy slipped into recession in the third quarter of 2024. The RBNZ noted that economic activity remains subdued, but lower rates and higher export earnings are expected to support a modest recovery this year.

"Lower interest rates will encourage spending, although elevated global economic uncertainty is expected to weigh on business investment decisions," it said, pointing to U.S. tariff policies.

The central bank sees more rate cuts this year "if economic conditions continue to evolve as projected." RBNZ Governor Adrian Orr projected two 25-bps cuts in April and May, with the rate reaching around 3% by year-end.

Consumer price inflation remains near the midpoint of its 1%-3% target band. "The economic outlook remains consistent with inflation remaining in the band over the medium term," the RBNZ said.

The kiwi dollar (NZD:USD) weakened against the greenback soon after the monetary policy move, but has since stabilized at around $0.572.

The RBNZ's decision comes a day after Australia's central bank reduced its cash rate for the first time since late 2020. "Early aggressive easing by the RBNZ suggests New Zealand can be better prepared to face trade headwinds than Australia," said Francesco Pesole, FX strategist, ING.

data.title: Straumann reports FY results
data.description:
• Straumann Holding AG press release (OTCPK:SAUHF): FY GAAP EPS of CHF 3.14.
  • Revenue of CHF 2.5B (+13.7%)
  • Fourth-quarter revenue showed 11.5% organic growth, amounting to CHF 645 million
  • Core EBIT margin at 26% including currency impact, or 27.6% at constant 2023 currencies, and core net profit at CHF 502 million
  • Implant system iEXCEL launched in key markets, digital platform AXS globally available
  • High employee engagement score of 82, with a 92% survey response rate
  • Board proposes further dividend increase to CHF 0.95
  • Outlook 2025: organic revenue growth in the high single-digit percentage range, with a 30 to 60 basis points improvement of the core EBIT margin at constant 2024 currency rates

data.title: Caledonia Mining names new finance chief
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (NYSE:CMCL) said that its CFO, Chester Goodburn, will step down from his role on 24 March 2025, following FY 2024 earnings release.

He will be succeeded, with immediate effect, by Ross Jerrard, subject to satisfaction of standard checks.

Mr Goodburn has served as Caledonia’s CFO since July 1, 2022, before which he had been Caledonia’s Group Financial Manager and Chief Information Officer.

Mr Jerrard, aged 50, brings extensive experience in financial leadership, having been the CFO of Centamin plc from April 2016 until its acquisition by AngloGold Ashanti.

data.title: Citigroup veteran Russell Chong to leave after ~27 years - report
